title: Bobblehead Day: When Heads Go Wiggle-Crazy!
story: Bobbleheads take over the world every January 7 on National Bobblehead Day! These funny little figures with their wobbly heads bouncing around celebrate everything from sports stars to movie characters. People show off their collections on social media, hunt for rare bobbleheads at stores, and even bring their favorites to work for good luck. Some fans organize bobblehead swaps with friends or visit museums that display giant collections. The best part? Give any bobblehead a tiny tap and watch that head dance like crazy for seconds!
activity_question: What happens when you tap a bobblehead?
activity_choices: Its head dances and wobbles around
It lights up with colorful LEDs
It spins in a complete circle
activity_answer: Its head dances and wobbles around
follow_up_question: What would you want your own custom bobblehead to look like?

title: Distaff Day: When Women Rule the Spindle!
story: Every January 7, women across Europe celebrate Distaff Day by returning to their spinning wheels after the Christmas holidays! A distaff is the wooden tool that holds wool or flax while spinning thread. Traditionally, this day marked when women got back to their regular work of making cloth and yarn. Today, crafters and fiber artists honor this old tradition by spinning, knitting, or weaving special projects. Some even share photos of their spinning wheels online with the hashtag #DistaffDay to keep this ancient craft alive!
activity_question: What is a distaff used for?
bonus_fact: In some places, men would playfully try to burn women's distaffs on this day!
